ddls:
CREATE TABLE "areas" (
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each area */,
  "area_name" TEXT /* Name of the area */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the area */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the area */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude of the area */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude of the area */,
  "area_type" TEXT /* Type of area (e.g., industrial, residential, agricultural) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., temperature, humidity, particulate) */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the sensor */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  "maintenance_schedule" TEXT /* Schedule for sensor maintenance */,
  "measurement_unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the sensor (e.g., degrees Celsius, percentage, micrograms per cubic meter) */,
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the area where the sensor is deployed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_area_id FOREIGN KEY ("area_id") REFERENCES areas ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "environmental_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that collected the data */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the data was collected */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time when the data was collected */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature reading in degrees Celsius */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity reading as a percentage */,
  "energy_usage" TEXT /* Energy usage reading in hexadecimal format */,
  "particulate" REAL /* Particulate matter reading in micrograms per cubic meter */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed reading in meters per second */,
  "atmospheric_pressure" REAL /* Atmospheric pressure reading in pascals */,
  "noise_pollution" REAL /* Noise pollution reading in decibels */,
  "data_collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the data (e.g., manual, automated) */,
  "data_quality_flag" TEXT /* Flag indicating data quality (e.g., valid, invalid, missing) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_environmental_data_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, analyst, observer) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or organization of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the data was accessed */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location from which the data was accessed (e.g., IP address, geographical coordinates) */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access event in seconds */,
  "data_volume_transferred" INTEGER /* Volume of data transferred during the access event in bytes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES environmental_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high temperature, low humidity) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Time when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "alert_resolution_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert resolution (e.g., open, in progress, resolved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES environmental_data ("data_id")
)
